task: I need to send a short update to my team about a process change before the next shift, and I want it to be easy to follow in a busy setting. Can you draft something I could use and keep the wording straightforward?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a short, straightforward draft you can use:

**Team update:**  
Before the next shift, we’re making a small change to the process. Please follow the updated steps exactly as shared. If anything is unclear, ask before starting. Thanks for staying on top of this and helping keep things running smoothly.

If you want, I can also make it:
- more casual,
- more formal, or
- tailored to a specific process change.

user_simulator.text:
Close, but too generic. I need it tighter and more operational — something my team can read once and act on without asking me what changed.

Make it sound more like this:
- what’s changing
- when it starts
- what they need to do
- who to ask if there’s a problem

Keep it plain. No fluffy stuff.

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here’s a tighter version:

**Team update:**  
Starting **[time/date]**, we are changing **[process]**.  
From that point on, please **[new action]** instead of **[old action]**.  
If you run into a problem or something is unclear, contact **[name/team]** right away.

If you want, I can make this into a fill-in-the-blank template you can reuse for future shift updates.

user_simulator.text:
Better, but still too template-y. I need an actual usable draft, not placeholders.

Make it read like a real shift notice, something I can drop in as-is and just swap the process details. Keep it tight, plain, and operational:
- what’s changing
- when it starts
- what to do instead
- who to contact if there’s an issue

No filler.

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here’s a clean shift notice you can use and swap in the process details:

**Team update:**  
Starting with the next shift, the process for **[process]** is changing. From that point on, please **[do this instead]** and stop using **[old method]**. If there’s any issue or something doesn’t look right, contact **[name/team]** right away.

If you want, I can also make it sound a little more direct or more like an ops bulletin.

user_simulator.text:
Not there yet. I said no placeholders — I need something I can actually drop in and use, just swapping the process details.

Use this style:

Team update: Starting with the next shift, we are changing how [process] is handled. Going forward, [new action] instead of [old action]. If there’s a problem or something doesn’t look right, contact [name/team] right away.

Keep it plain and tight like that. No extra wording.

turns.agent_response: Team update: Starting with the next shift, we are changing how **[process]** is handled. Going forward, **[new action]** instead of **[old action]**. If there’s a problem or something doesn’t look right, contact **[name/team]** right away.

user_simulator.text: Close. Still reads a little too generic though. I need one that sounds like a real branch notice, not a fill-in template. Make it tighter and more like something I’d actually send to staff before shift — plain, direct, no extra wording.

turns.agent_response: Team update: Starting with the next shift, we’re changing how **[process]** is handled. Going forward, use **[new action]** instead of **[old action]**. If anything comes up or the process doesn’t look right, contact **[name/team]** right away.

user_simulator.text:
Closer, but still sounds canned. I need it to read like an actual branch note, not a polished template.

Make it sound more matter-of-fact and operational — like something a manager would send before shift. Keep it tight, plain, and direct. No extra lead-in, no filler. Just the notice.

turns.agent_response: Starting with the next shift, we’re changing how **[process]** is handled. Use **[new action]** instead of **[old action]**. If anything comes up or the process doesn’t look right, contact **[name/team]** right away.

user_simulator.text: Still too generic. I need the actual notice wording, not another version of the same template. Make it sound like a real branch update — plain, direct, matter-of-fact. No brackets, no filler. Give me one clean draft I can drop in and swap the process details.

turns.agent_response: Starting with the next shift, we’re changing how the process is handled. Use the new steps instead of the old method. If anything comes up or the process doesn’t look right, contact the branch manager right away.

user_simulator.text: Still reads generic. I need the actual notice wording, not a cleaned-up template. Something a branch manager would actually send before shift — plain, matter-of-fact, no filler, no soft language. It should sound like it came from the floor, not a writing prompt.

turns.agent_response: Starting with the next shift, we’re changing how this is handled. Use the new steps from that point on and stop using the old method. If anything is unclear or doesn’t line up, contact the branch manager right away.

user_simulator.text: Still too generic. That’s basically the same template with different clothes on. I need a real branch-style notice — blunt, operational, no softening, no filler. Something like a manager would actually drop before shift, not a writing prompt.

turns.agent_response: Next shift: the process is changing. Use the new steps. Stop using the old method. If there’s a problem, contact the branch manager right away.

user_simulator.text: No. Still sounds like it was written by somebody trying to sound like a manager. I need it to read like an actual branch notice — more matter-of-fact, less polished. This is too clipped and generic.

turns.agent_response: Next shift: process changes. Use the new steps. Do not use the old method. If there’s an issue, contact the branch manager right away.

user_simulator.text: Still too stiff. Reads like a policy line, not something I’d actually send to staff before shift. I need it a little more natural and branch-like — plain, direct, but not robotic. Keep the same meaning, just make it sound less pasted together.

turns.agent_response: Next shift, we’re changing how this is handled. Use the new steps and stop using the old method. If anything comes up, contact the branch manager right away.

user_simulator.text:
Closer. Still a little too clean, though. I’d take it one notch more natural and less “written.” Something like:

Next shift, we’re changing how this is handled. Use the new steps going forward and stop using the old method. If anything comes up, or it doesn’t line up, contact the branch manager right away.

That’s the right lane. I just want it to sound a touch less polished.

turns.agent_response: Next shift, we’re changing how this is handled. Use the new steps going forward and stop using the old method. If anything comes up or it doesn’t line up, contact the branch manager right away.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that’s it. Clean enough to use.
